picks.headline: Big Barren Creek: water, soil, timber all present
picks.reason: 385 acres with Big Barren Creek providing year-round water plus multiple ponds, agricultural-suitable soil, timber for harvest, and grazing land. At $2,273/acre this is genuinely good value for the resource density. No restrictions noted; proximity to Van Buren provides services without sacrificing rural character.

picks.headline: 283 acres, cabin included, lowest price per acre
picks.reason: At $1,498/acre with an existing cabin and Macks Creek water access, this is the best price-per-acre on the list for a property with shelter and water. Off-grid viable and large enough for serious food production. Caveat: road access quality and utility infrastructure are unconfirmed and must be verified.

picks.headline: 520 acres near Black River, no restrictions
picks.reason: 520 acres at $2,000/acre is exceptional scale for the price in southeast Missouri. Direct road access, proximity to Black River and Clearwater Lake for water, and no HOA or noted restrictions. Utilities and soil specifics are unconfirmed, but the acreage and water access make this a strong raw-land homestead candidate.

picks.headline: 358 Ozark acres, springs, well, and highway access
picks.reason: Multiple natural springs plus an existing well solve the water problem across 358 acres at $2,360/acre. Direct highway access and strong timber and hunting potential with no HOA. Key unknowns are utility availability and zoning, but the water infrastructure and scale at this price are hard to match.

picks.headline: 180 acres, electric service and live water in place
picks.reason: 180 acres with on-site 20A–50A electric, live water, and a well already installed removes the two biggest off-grid startup barriers at $2,749/acre. Off-grid viable with hunting and agricultural potential. Road access and residential zoning need verification, but the infrastructure head start is significant.

picks.headline: 80 Ozark acres with electric, well, and pond
picks.reason: Electric on-site, a working well, and a pond provide water independence on a buildable 80-acre Ozarks parcel at $2,499/acre. No HOA, near Norfork Lake, and timber potential round out the fundamentals. Main unknowns are soil quality and zoning detail, but the utilities package is already solved.

picks.headline: 250 acres, creek plus spring, home and shop ready
picks.reason: Year-round creek and spring water on 250 acres with an existing home and shop significantly reduces startup effort. Agricultural soil suitable for Ozark conditions supports grazing and diversified production at $3,498/acre. Terrain is rolling rather than row-crop flat, but that is typical and manageable for homesteading.

picks.headline: 227 acres, spring-fed water, house and paved access
picks.reason: Spring-fed year-round water, 227 acres of pasture and timber adjacent to Mark Twain National Forest, an existing house, and paved access via Wayne 421 at $2,973/acre. No HOA. Utility and zoning confirmation is still needed, but the natural water and existing structure make this a strong turnkey candidate.

picks.headline: 750-acre turnkey ranch, homes and water secured
picks.reason: Modern homes, utilities, and Strawberry River access already in place across 750 acres at $3,313/acre with no HOA. Agnos silt loam soil (class 6) rules out row crops but supports grazing and forestry well—this is a livestock-focused homestead, not a market garden. High total price ($2.48M) is the principal barrier.

picks.headline: 67 acres with spring, creek, well, and septic installed
picks.reason: Spring and creek provide dual water sources, and both well and septic are already in place on 67.5 acres at $4,133/acre—removing the two most expensive infrastructure items. Mixed timber and open ground support harvesting and cultivation. Road access and electricity availability are not confirmed and should be checked.

picks.headline: 60 Ozark acres, pond, pasture, and hardwood timber
picks.reason: Established pond, pasture, and hardwood timber on 60 acres with agricultural-suitable soil at $3,750/acre. Year-round water noted, no major restrictions identified. A smaller but genuinely productive parcel; well and septic status should be verified before closing.

picks.headline: 53 Arkansas acres with creek, pond, and springs
picks.reason: Three distinct water sources—creek, pond, and springs—on 53 acres at $4,000/acre with paved access and off-grid viable designation. Agricultural potential and good Ozarks location. Soil quality, zoning, and on-site utility availability are not confirmed, keeping this ranked lower despite the impressive water profile.
